$schema: Manifest ↔ Prisma schema-drift allowlist. See manifest/scripts/audit-schema-drift.mjs for semantics.

_doc.globalAdapterDerived: Cross-cutting fields that EVERY Prisma store adapter injects from runtime context, not from command input. Applies to every governed entity.
_doc.adapterDerived: Per-entity fields that the entity's Prisma store adapter derives via a documented rule (e.g. looking up a parent entity).
_doc.nonconforming: Per constitution §14, acknowledged tech debt with an active removal plan. Bypass is not permission.
_doc.ignoredEntities: Entity names to skip entirely (e.g. infrastructure tables incorrectly tagged as governed).
_doc.ignoredFields: Per-entity field allowlist for fields the audit should not enforce.

tenantId.rule: Every Prisma store adapter injects tenantId from RuntimeContext.user.tenantId. See manifest/runtime/src/prisma-store.ts where each .create() sets `tenantId: this.tenantId`. Constitution §19 requires runtime tenant context on every governed command.

tenant_id.rule: snake_case variant of tenantId for legacy snake_case models (ActionMilestone, DisciplinaryAction, OnboardingCompletion, OnboardingTask, PerformanceReview, EventFollowup, ...). Every Prisma store adapter injects tenantId from RuntimeContext.user.tenantId; the prisma-model-metadata bridge maps the IR tenantId property onto the tenant_id column. Constitution §19.

id.rule: The Prisma store adapter generates id via crypto.randomUUID() when the create command does not supply one (see manifest/runtime/src/prisma-store.ts .create()). Most entities take id as an explicit create param; no-param definition creates (e.g. SelOnboardingTraining*Definition) rely on the generated id.

transferNumber.rule: Generated server-side by InventoryTransferPrismaStore.create in manifest/runtime/src/prisma-stores/inventory-transfer-prisma-store.ts. Pattern: `TRF-${count+1, zero-padded to 6}` where count is tenant-scoped prisma.inventoryTransfer.count(). The UI does not send transferNumber; the manifest does not declare it as a create param. Matches the contract in apps/api/__tests__/inventory/transfers/transfers.quarantine.test.ts.

requestedBy.rule: Adapter-derived from RuntimeContext.user.id. Plumbed via createPrismaStoreProvider's userId arg (manifest/runtime/src/prisma-store.ts) -> InventoryTransferPrismaStore constructor's requestedBy arg -> tx.inventoryTransfer.create({ data: { requestedBy: this.requestedBy || null } }). Prisma column is nullable; we explicitly never silently fall back to empty string per IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md.

items.rule: Manifest declares items as string with default '[]' because the manifest IR grammar has no array<object> kind. The dispatcher passes the UI's items array (or a server-to-server JSON string) verbatim; InventoryTransferPrismaStore.asTransferItems normalizes both shapes and fans out to inventory_transfer_items rows inside the same $transaction as the parent row. Same grammar-blocked pattern as ClientPreference.preferenceValue + CommandBoardLayout.viewport.

preferenceValue.rule: Manifest IR grammar cannot express Json/object types. Manifest declares preferenceValue as string; the dispatcher serializes/deserializes JSON transparently. Prisma column is @db.Json. Same grammar-blocked pattern as InventoryTransfer.items and CommandBoardLayout.viewport.

viewport.rule: Manifest IR grammar cannot express Json/object types. Manifest declares viewport as string with default '{}'; the dispatcher passes the JSON object verbatim. Prisma column is @db.Json. Same grammar-blocked pattern as InventoryTransfer.items and ClientPreference.preferenceValue.

permissions.rule: Manifest IR grammar cannot express Json/array-of-object types. Manifest declares permissions as string; the dispatcher passes the array verbatim which Prisma stores as Json. Prisma column is @db.Json.

trigger_type.rule: Adapter-derived from the manifest command context. The workflow engine sets trigger_type based on the event that triggered the workflow creation. Prisma column is String.

permissions.rule: Manifest IR grammar cannot express Json/array-of-object types. Manifest declares permissions as array; the dispatcher serializes the array to Json for Prisma storage. Same grammar-blocked pattern as Workflow.permissions.

startTime.rule: Manifest declares startTime as string (HH:MM format). GenericPrismaStore coerces to Date for Prisma @db.DateTime column via new Date(`1970-01-01T${startTime}`). See memory: GenericPrismaStore @db.Time/@db.Date coercion drift.

endTime.rule: Manifest declares endTime as string (HH:MM format). GenericPrismaStore coerces to Date for Prisma @db.DateTime column via new Date(`1970-01-01T${endTime}`). Same coercion pattern as startTime.

issuedDate.rule: Manifest declares issuedDate as string (ISO date). GenericPrismaStore coerces to Date for Prisma @db.DateTime column. Same coercion pattern as EmployeeAvailability.startTime.

periodStart.rule: Manifest declares periodStart as string (ISO date). GenericPrismaStore coerces to Date for Prisma @db.DateTime column. Same coercion pattern as EmployeeAvailability.startTime.

periodEnd.rule: Manifest declares periodEnd as string (ISO date). GenericPrismaStore coerces to Date for Prisma @db.DateTime column. Same coercion pattern as EmployeeAvailability.startTime.

effectiveDate.rule: Manifest declares effectiveDate as string (ISO date). GenericPrismaStore coerces to Date for Prisma @db.DateTime column. Same coercion pattern as EmployeeAvailability.startTime.

startDate.rule: Manifest declares startDate as string (ISO date). GenericPrismaStore coerces to Date for Prisma @db.DateTime column. Same coercion pattern as EmployeeAvailability.startTime.

endDate.rule: Manifest declares endDate as string (ISO date). GenericPrismaStore coerces to Date for Prisma @db.DateTime column. Same coercion pattern as EmployeeAvailability.startTime.

hours.rule: Adapter-derived: computed from startDate/endDate difference during business hours, or supplied by payroll integration. Prisma column is Decimal. TODO: consider adding as explicit create param for employee self-service.

recipeId.rule: Parent-context propagated from the parent Recipe entity. RecipeVersion belongs to Recipe and inherits recipeId server-side. The create command does not take recipeId as user input.

name.rule: Adapter-derived: inherited from the parent Recipe's name when creating a version. The version carries the same name as its parent recipe.

versionNumber.rule: Adapter-derived: auto-computed as max(versionNumber) + 1 for the tenant and recipe. First version gets 1. The create command does not take versionNumber as user input.

assignedBy.rule: Adapter-derived from RuntimeContext.user.id. The currently authenticated user performing the training assignment. Same pattern as InventoryTransfer.requestedBy.

slug.rule: Auto-generated from the title field by the store adapter (slugified: title.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')). Prisma requires slug as unique String.

utilization.rule: Computed from thresholdPct/actualPct by the BudgetAlert adapter. The manifest create takes thresholdPct and alertType; utilization is derived server-side.

name.rule: Adapter-derived: the labor budget name is generated from the period and type (e.g. 'Weekly Budget - Week 23, 2026'). Manifest create does not take name as user input.

budgetTarget.rule: Adapter-derived: mapped from manifest budgetAmount field. Prisma uses budgetTarget, manifest uses budgetAmount — the adapter renames the field during persistence.

budgetUnit.rule: Adapter-derived: inferred from budgetType (e.g. weekly→hours, monthly→dollars). Manifest create takes budgetType; budgetUnit is resolved server-side.

versionNumber.rule: Hardcoded to 1 in manifest create via mutate. Subsequent versions use a different command. Correct initial value.

changeSummary.rule: Adapter-derived: mapped from manifest changeDescription field. Prisma uses changeSummary, manifest uses changeDescription — the adapter renames the field during persistence.

documentType.rule: Adapter-derived: inferred from the parent document entity's type during creation. The document version inherits its type from the parent document.

eventId.rule: Parent-context propagated from the linked Invoice. Manifest source explicitly documents this: eventId + clientId are NOT caller input, inherited server-side from Invoice.

clientId.rule: Parent-context propagated from the linked Invoice. Same as eventId — inherited server-side from Invoice.

locationId.rule: Parent-context propagated from the parent Schedule entity. ScheduleShift belongs to Schedule and inherits locationId server-side. The ScheduleShift.create command does not accept locationId as input — it is resolved from the linked Schedule. See Task 8.10, commit 1bba3a9f9.

status.rule: Hardcoded to 'pending' in manifest create via mutate status = 'pending'. Initial status of an approval request is always pending, never user-supplied.

query_config.rule: Json column `query_config Json` (required, no default) is fed by the IR string property queryConfig (default "{}") via create/update string params. The manifest grammar cannot express Json/object types; the store adapter JSON-parses the string param into Prisma Json input — established ClientPreference.preferenceValue pattern. Sibling display_config Json is the same shape (unflagged only because it has @default).

id.rule: id is generated by the Manifest runtime's createInstance bootstrap and persisted by GenericPrismaStore as part of the compound PK [tenantId, id]; it is never a create param. EventStaff is a legacy Task 0.3-style model (camelCase physical columns, no @map, no relations) whose id column uniquely lacks @default(dbgenerated("gen_random_uuid()")), so the audit cannot see a DB default — but the value is always supplied by the adapter.

GenericPrismaStore injects the id at create: 'id uses the supplied id or a fresh uuid' (manifest/runtime/dist/prisma-stores/generic-prisma-store.d.ts). The Prisma column has no inline @id (composite @@id([tenantId, id])) so the audit's id exemption misses it.

createdAt.rule: GenericPrismaStore fills required DateTime columns lacking a DB default with `new Date()` ('the createdAt convention', generic-prisma-store.d.ts). StaffMember.createdAt is `DateTime` with no @default(now()), unlike sibling models, so the audit flags it even though the adapter always supplies it.

updatedAt.rule: Same GenericPrismaStore rule: `updatedAt DateTime` on StaffMember has neither @updatedAt nor @default, so the audit's isUpdatedAt exemption does not fire; the store fills required DateTime columns without a DB default with new Date() on create.

startTime.reason: Column is `startTime DateTime @map("start_time") @db.Time(6)` NOT NULL. Senders DELIBERATELY send ISO strings (apps/app/app/(authenticated)/scheduling/availability/actions.ts:258-273, with an in-code comment explaining that GenericPrismaStore coerces string params via new Date() and a bare "HH:MM" would NULL the NOT NULL Time column). Retyping the IR property to datetime would force epoch-ms params (engine rejects ISO with E_TYPE_DATETIME) and break the working ISO-string senders. This entity IS the EmployeeAvailability ISO-string-coercion precedent.

endTime.reason: Same as startTime: `endTime DateTime @map("end_time") @db.Time(6)` NOT NULL fed by deliberate ISO-string params from availability/actions.ts (documented in-code). EmployeeAvailability ISO-string coercion precedent.

effectiveFrom.reason: `effectiveFrom DateTime @default(dbgenerated("CURRENT_DATE")) @map("effective_from") @db.Date` fed by ISO-string params (actions.ts:272 createEffectiveFrom2.toISOString()). @db.Date column + string-prop + store coercion = EmployeeAvailability precedent.

effectiveUntil.reason: `effectiveUntil DateTime? @map("effective_until") @db.Date` fed by ISO string OR "" (actions.ts:273 — "" maps to null via GenericPrismaStore asNullableDate). The empty-string-means-null idiom only works with a string-typed IR property; datetime would reject it. EmployeeAvailability precedent.

issuedDate.reason: `issuedDate DateTime @map("issued_date") @db.Date` NOT NULL fed by string params from live routes (apps/api/app/api/staff/certifications/route.ts:113 `issuedDate: rawBody.issuedDate || rawBody.issued_date`). @db.Date + string-prop + GenericPrismaStore asNullableDate coercion = the EmployeeAvailability ISO-string precedent. Retyping to datetime would reject the existing ISO/date-string senders (E_TYPE_DATETIME).

expiryDate.reason: `expiryDate DateTime? @map("expiry_date") @db.Date` fed by string-or-"" params (certifications/[id]/route.ts:80 and route.ts:114 send `expiryDate: ... || ""`; "" coerces to null via asNullableDate). The empty-string-means-null idiom requires a string-typed property. EmployeeAvailability precedent.

tags.reason: Intentional adapter-bridged divergence: the bespoke KitchenTaskPrismaStore normalizes the IR's comma-string representation to the Postgres String[] column via toStringArray (manifest/runtime/src/prisma-store.ts:374 create, :420 update; helper at :1070 documents 'manifest values may arrive as an array OR a legacy comma-string'). The IR commands addTag/removeTag implement comma-string semantics (`self.tags + "," + tag`, removeTagFromString builtin at manifest/runtime/src/manifest-builtins.ts:102) and the UI sends tags as a string (apps/app/app/(authenticated)/kitchen/tasks/actions.ts:150 `tags: ""`). Same store-coercion pattern as Station.equipmentList (prisma-store.ts:852).

periodStart.reason: Column is DateTime? @db.Date; active senders pass ISO strings (labor-budget.ts:260 'periodStart.toISOString()' into create, :324 into update). String-typed props let the store layer coerce ISO strings into Date (EmployeeAvailability @db.Date/@db.Time precedent); retyping to datetime would make the runtime reject these ISO-string senders with E_TYPE_DATETIME.

periodEnd.reason: Same as periodStart: DateTime? @db.Date column fed by ISO strings from labor-budget.ts:261/:325; string prop coerces fine via the store, datetime retype would E_TYPE_DATETIME the live senders (EmployeeAvailability precedent).

effectiveDate.reason: Column is DateTime @db.Date (PascalCase table tenant_staff.EmployeeDeduction); senders pass date strings — e.g. apps/api/__tests__/scheduling/schedules.test.ts:521 sends effectiveDate: "2026-05-04", and create/update/deactivate params are string-typed. EmployeeAvailability precedent: store coerces ISO date strings for string props; retyping to datetime forces epoch-ms and rejects these senders with E_TYPE_DATETIME.

endDate.reason: Same as effectiveDate: DateTime? @db.Date column, string params in create/update/deactivate (which sets endDate when deactivating), ISO-date-string senders. EmployeeAvailability @db.Date precedent.

periodStart.reason: Column is DateTime @db.Date; the live sender apps/api/app/api/payroll/periods/route.ts:152 passes periodStart: rawBody.periodStart || "" (ISO date string) into the string-typed create param. EmployeeAvailability @db.Date precedent: string prop + store-layer ISO coercion; datetime retype would E_TYPE_DATETIME the active sender.

periodEnd.reason: Same as periodStart: DateTime @db.Date column fed by ISO date strings from the periods route; string param coerces via the store (EmployeeAvailability precedent).

runDate.reason: Column is DateTime @default(now()) @db.Timestamptz(6); the live governed sender apps/api/lib/payroll/manifest-payroll-data-source.ts:166 passes runDate: new Date().toISOString() — an ISO string into the string-typed create param. Store coerces ISO strings for string props; retyping to datetime would reject this active sender with E_TYPE_DATETIME (EmployeeAvailability coercion precedent extended to Timestamptz).

startDate.reason: @db.Date column fed by string senders: apps/app/app/(authenticated)/scheduling/time-off/actions.ts createTimeOffRequest sends `startDate: input.startDate` (a date string validated via new Date()). String-typed IR props are ISO-coerced fine by the store layer, while retyping to datetime would force epoch-ms params (E_TYPE_DATETIME) and break the live caller plus the `self.startDate != ""` constraint/guards. EmployeeAvailability @db.Time/@db.Date precedent.

endDate.reason: Identical to startDate: `endDate DateTime @map("end_date") @db.Date` column with string senders from scheduling/time-off/actions.ts and `self.endDate != ""` constraint; ISO-string coercion at the store layer (EmployeeAvailability precedent), datetime retype would reject the live caller's strings.

items.reason: Adapter fan-out pattern, intentionally column-less: InventoryTransferPrismaStore.asTransferItems accepts a real array or JSON string, then persists [{itemId, quantity, notes}] rows to the inventory_transfer_items child table inside the same $transaction as the parent row (manifest/runtime/src/prisma-stores/inventory-transfer-prisma-store.ts:103-141); reads re-include child rows and surface them as items. IR grammar has no array<object> kind (same rationale as ClientPreference.preferenceValue Json-as-string precedent). The in-source comment documents this.

assignedTo.reason: Assignment state intentionally persists as tenant_kitchen.task_claims rows, not a kitchen_tasks column: the app uses a documented two-step flow (KitchenTask.claim sets status+assignedTo, then KitchenTaskClaim.claim creates the claim record — apps/app/app/(authenticated)/kitchen/tasks/actions.ts:382-424; release is the mirror at :437-501). KitchenTaskPrismaStore derives the current assignee from the active claim on every read (prisma-store.ts:355-361, :524).

claimedBy.reason: Adapter-derived and adapter-persisted via tenant_kitchen.task_claims: PrepTaskPrismaStore.update translates claimedBy changes into claim-record lifecycle (create new claim / release on unclaim / release+create on reassign — manifest/runtime/src/prisma-store.ts:174-224) and reads hydrate claimedBy from the active claim's employeeId (:96-102, :274). Conformance covered by apps/api/__tests__/kitchen/manifest-preptask-claim-conformance.test.ts.

claimedAt.reason: Same adapter channel as claimedBy: PrepTaskPrismaStore writes claimedAt into the task_claims record (prisma-store.ts:138, :188-190, :219-221) and reads it back from the active claim (:275-277).
